name: Harissa (Moroccan Hot Sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cumin, coriander, cardamom, chili pequins, cloves, and black peppercorns in a small saucepan.
2. Toast the spices over moderately high heat for 3-4 minutes, shaking the pan frequently to prevent burning.
3. Allow the spices to cool for 2-3 minutes, then transfer them to a mortar.
4. Crush all the ingredients thoroughly with a pestle until they form a paste.
5. Add cayenne pepper and salt, then crush everything together into a smooth mixture.
6. Transfer the paste to a small jar.
7. Pour in olive oil and cover tightly.
8. Store at room temperature. The sauce will keep for several weeks.
9. Use sparingly when flavoring hot sauces for couscous, meatballs, and meat loaves. Each time you use it, replenish the oil so that the spice mixture is always covered by about 1/3 of oil.
